title: calceolaria × quinamadivensis
intro: Calceolaria × quinamadivensis, also known as the Slipper Flower, is a hybrid plant species in the Calceolaria genus, native to the Andes mountains of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammatory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Calceolaria × quinamadivensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders and other health issues.
